replies.author: @bigblue207
replies.text:
@robbo_96 I think it’s just the heavy handiness of it. Like obviously if things were that simple it wouldn’t be a problem. Superman did the exact thing. Actually, Superman did it worse, because it itself introduced the idea that “hey maybe there’s actually more to it than just killing a bad guy” and they did it anyway. 

Both movies had this weird issue where on the surface, they were trying to revive the Saturday morning cartoon vibe of simple atmosphere, simple plot, simple issues. But then both dipped their toe into something a bit more philosophically complex and then said “no, just kidding”. Spiderverse kind of did it too. All three introduced some variation of the trolley paradox, the point of which is there is no right answer, you lose out in any case or someone does. But then they decided “no guys, you just stop the trolley 🤯” which like wow thanks jackass that completely misses the entire point of the paradox. And everyone in the audience goes “oh yeah! They’re superheroes! It’s that easy!” 

Me personally if they’re going to take it upon themselves to introduce a more complex idea and then entirely abandon even exploring it to its full potential, I think that’s cowardice. They didn’t have to introduce this moral ambiguity. They thought they would make it more interesting. But ultimately it was a tool they misused. It’s like doing surgery with a sledgehammer. 

And the disturbing part is how it’s reinforcing this simple take in the real world. “Superman said we should just kill evil people, so why aren’t we doing it yet? Fantastic four and Spiderverse said family come first, fuck everyone else, and I agree so that makes me a good person!” Like no, if those things were true then the tension within those films is completely meaningless. It comes across really impulsive and tactless. 

What Lois was bringing up and trying to get at was killing the leader of a country leaves power vacuums and strains international tensions and in the real world, you can get a lot more innocent people caught up in a conflict that they never needed to be in. In Spiderverse and FF, the drama comes from the conflict that saving your family might doom the rest of the entire world, and you have no guarantee that saving your family actually works in the end anyway. Actually miles is doing the exact same thing kingpin was doing, risking the universe to try and keep his own happy family together, but the framing is from the opposite perspective and it’s actually really bizarre that no one is realizing that. It says to me a lot of times these days people need to have their opinions instructed to them instead of actually taking it upon themselves to think through things critically and arrive at their own conclusion. I suppose that’s not really surprising but it is pretty disappointing I think

replies.author: @WunHitWonder
replies.text: @powderkegg LMAO in your opinion. And I have not seen anyone bring up Minus 1. However, purely from a business standpoint, the numbers (assuming the higher 15 mil budget and lower 113 mil box office revenue to favor the doubtful), that movie made 7.5x its budget. Let's just assume the movie used 15 mil for marketing (again - for doubters): that is 30 million to 113 million. That would mean it still made 3.75x its budget. These American made films are not made for an audience they've long lost and they pump them up as something they're not. The audience is smarter and tired of being lied to by the media, influencers, and people who plain promote anything Marvel/Disney.  Comparatively, The Fantastic Four: First Steps, had a production budget of approximately $200 million. To be considered a box office success, Hollywood blockbusters typically need to earn 2 to 2.5 times their production budget. This means "The Fantastic Four: First Steps" needs to gross between $400 million and $500 million worldwide to break even, including production and marketing costs. It is currently sitting at $211,963,853 and is expected to hit 500 million. Now, I hope that is not true because I really would like a comic book movie to succeed, but Disney needs to change it up and abandon the way they have been doing things the last 5 years, because it stinks.

replies.author: @GideonRavenor712
replies.text:
Exactly.

Even if the films are technically horror movies, they're generally not explicit enough to turn parents off of their kids demanding to see Dinosaurs.

Plus, one can argue ever since Jurassic Park 1, the writing has gone downhill, but ESPECIALLY with the "World" series. If those people are still watching the series after the first two Jurassic Worlds (I had issues with the first, but Fallen Kingdom was my cut off they just completely pissed me off), they're not turned off by the absolute stupidity in the writing.

Like the Godzilla movies, similar sort of thing there too. Abysmal writing, but people go to see it because they want to see giant monsters fight in good CGI.

Now, Superman is one of the three tentpoles of Comic Heroes, so him having more staying power makes sense. Him along with Batman and Spider-Man are the top 3, and you can reasonably say something will initially sell well (even if it drops off fast) off of their names alone.

But Fantastic Four has never sold exceptionally well in film, nor has it been a primary driver in the comics even if they are well-recognized or have a decent fanbase. As Gary has pointed out, the best Fantastic Four film has been The Incredibles.

replies.author: @Meatball-Mike
replies.text: True, I hate him, although I did watch the movie and liked it. Also I don’t get how people think sue storm had a girl boss moment, she used her telekinesis to push galactus, she’s like the only one who can effectively do that, not to mention it killed her for a short period of time. There was nothing wrong with that scene and it wasn’t “fEmAlE LeD”, they all played a major part in that, the thing nocked down any building galactus held on to, Mr fantastic both distracted galactus and saved Franklin, and are we just gonna forget how the human torch was literally gonna sacrifice himself because sue couldn’t push galactus any further? The “critics” on that part are literally mad because sue actually did something🫩. Pedro aside, the movie was actually good, the only other complaints I see is on the silver surfer being a woman, any actual marvel fan would know that it’s an actual character, separate from the one we know. The only valid complaints I’ve seen so far is with Pedro, other than that it was just people searching for things to complain about that wasn’t Pedro. This makes me genuinely disappointed in people because they will let one thing ruin the entirety of something
